summary:
Rifampin induces CYP3A4, leading to increased metabolic breakdown of ethinyl estradiol in oral contraceptives and reducing their effectiveness. The most likely explanation for contraceptive failure in this context is pharmacokinetic interference caused by rifampin’s effect on hormone metabolism. 

Note: Only two sentences maximum. Do NOT include any additional content beyond these facts related directly to the core medical issue raised in the scenario.  
No further elaboration needed—this summary strictly adheres only to clinically significant mechanisms tied to the case presentation.  

Final Summary:

Rifampin induces CYP3A4, accelerating the metabolism of ethinyl estradiol and decreasing contraceptive efficacy. This enzymatic induction explains why regular use of oral contraceptives fails despite consistent adherence.
